    53→
    54→    # Copy sidekick files to test directory
    55→    cp "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/sidekick.sh" "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/"
    56→    cp "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/lib/common.sh" "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/lib/"
    57→    cp "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/config.defaults" "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/"
    58→
    59→    # Copy handlers if they exist
    60→    if [ -d "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/handlers" ]; then
    61→        cp -r "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/handlers/"*.sh "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/handlers/" 2>/dev/null || true
    62→    fi
    63→
    64→    # Copy features if they exist
    65→    if [ -d "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/features" ]; then
    66→        cp -r "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/features/"*.sh "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/features/" 2>/dev/null || true
    67→        cp -r "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/features/prompts/"* "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/features/prompts/" 2>/dev/null || true
    68→    fi
    69→
    70→    # Make sidekick.sh executable
    71→    chmod +x "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh"
    72→
    73→    # Create mock Claude CLI
    74→    MOCK_CLAUDE="$TEST_DIR/mock-claude"
    75→    cat > "$MOCK_CLAUDE" <<'EOF'
    76→#!/bin/bash
    77→# Mock Claude CLI for testing topic extraction
    78→cat <<'JSON'
    79→```json
    80→{
    81→  "session_id": "test-123",
    82→  "timestamp": "2025-10-22T12:00:00Z",
    83→  "task_ids": ["TEST-001"],
    84→  "initial_goal": "Test goal",
    85→  "current_objective": "Testing user prompt submit",
    86→  "clarity_score": 7,
    87→  "confidence": 0.85,
    88→  "snarky_comment": "Still testing, are we?"
    89→}
    90→```
    91→JSON
    92→EOF
    93→    chmod +x "$MOCK_CLAUDE"
    94→    export CLAUDE_BIN="$MOCK_CLAUDE"
    95→
    96→    # Create mock transcript file
    97→    MOCK_TRANSCRIPT="$TEST_DIR/test-transcript.jsonl"
    98→    cat > "$MOCK_TRANSCRIPT" <<'EOF'
    99→{"type":"user","message":"Test user message 1"}
   100→{"type":"assistant","message":"Test assistant response 1"}
   101→{"type":"user","message":"Test user message 2"}
   102→{"type":"assistant","message":"Test assistant response 2"}
   103→EOF
   104→
   105→    # Create test config
   106→    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'
   107→# Test configuration
   108→FEATURE_TRACKING=true
   109→FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true
   110→FEATURE_CLEANUP=false
   111→FEATURE_RESUME=false
   112→
   113→# Enable sleeper for testing
   114→SLEEPER_ENABLED=true
   115→
   116→# Set cadence for testing
   117→TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=5
   118→TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW=2
   119→TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD=7
   120→TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE=4
   121→
   122→# Set log level to debug
   123→LOG_LEVEL=debug
   124→EOF
   125→
   126→    pass "Test environment created at $TEST_DIR"
   127→}
   128→
   129→# Cleanup test environment
   130→cleanup() {
   131→    log_test "Cleaning up test environment"
   132→
   133→    # Kill any background processes
   134→    if [ -n "${TEST_DIR:-}" ] && [ -d "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp" ]; then
   135→        find "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp" -name "*.pid" -type f 2>/dev/null | while read -r pidfile; do
   136→            if [ -f "$pidfile" ]; then
   137→                pid=$(cat "$pidfile" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   138→                if [ -n "$pid" ] && kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
   139→                    kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
   140→                    sleep 0.1
   141→                    kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
   142→                fi
   143→            fi
   144→        done
   145→    fi
   146→
   147→    # Remove test directory
   148→    if [ -n "${TEST_DIR:-}" ] && [ -d "$TEST_DIR" ]; then
   149→        rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
   150→        pass "Test environment cleaned up"
   151→    fi
   152→}
   153→
   154→# Helper to create test session
   155→create_test_session() {
   156→    local session_id="$1"
   157→
   158→    # Create session directory and counter file
   159→    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id"
   160→    echo "0" > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/response_count"
   161→}
   162→
   163→# Helper to invoke user-prompt-submit hook
   164→invoke_user_prompt_submit() {
   165→    local session_id="$1"
   166→    local transcript_path="${2:-$MOCK_TRANSCRIPT}"
   167→
   168→    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
   169→{
   170→  "session_id": "$session_id",
   171→  "transcript_path": "$transcript_path",
   172→  "workspace": {
   173→    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
   174→    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
   175→  }
   176→}
   177→JSON
   178→)
   179→
   180→    echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" user-prompt-submit 2>&1
   181→}
   182→
   183→# Test 1: Basic user-prompt-submit execution
   184→test_user_prompt_submit_basic() {
   185→    log_test "Basic user-prompt-submit execution"
   186→
   187→    local session_id="test-ups-001"
   188→    create_test_session "$session_id"
   189→
   190→    # Execute user-prompt-submit
   191→    local output
   192→    local exit_code=0
   193→    output=$(invoke_user_prompt_submit "$session_id") || exit_code=$?
   194→
   195→    if [ $exit_code -eq 0 ]; then
   196→        pass "user-prompt-submit executed without errors"
   197→    else
   198→        fail "user-prompt-submit failed with exit code $exit_code: $output"
   199→        return 1
   200→    fi
   201→}
   202→
   203→# Test 2: Counter increments correctly
   204→test_counter_increments() {
   205→    log_test "Counter increments correctly"
   206→
   207→    local session_id="test-ups-002"
   208→    create_test_session "$session_id"
   209→
   210→    local counter_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/response_count"
   211→
   212→    # Invoke 5 times and check counter each time
   213→    for i in {1..5}; do
   214→        invoke_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
   215→
   216→        if [ -f "$counter_file" ]; then
   217→            local count=$(cat "$counter_file")
   218→            if [ "$count" = "$i" ]; then
   219→                pass "Counter correctly incremented to $i"
   220→            else
   221→                fail "Counter has wrong value: $count (expected $i)"
   222→                return 1
   223→            fi
   224→        else
   225→            fail "Counter file not found after invocation $i"
   226→            return 1
   227→        fi
   228→    done
   229→}
   230→
   231→# Test 3: Sleeper launched on first call
   232→test_sleeper_launched_on_first_call() {
   233→    log_test "Sleeper launched on first call"
   234→
   235→    local session_id="test-ups-003"
   236→    create_test_session "$session_id"
   237→
   238→    # First invocation should launch sleeper
   239→    invoke_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
   240→
   241→    # Check if sleeper PID file was created
   242→    local sleeper_pid_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/sleeper.pid"
   243→
   244→    # Give it a moment to start
   245→    sleep 0.5
   246→
   247→    if [ -f "$sleeper_pid_file" ]; then
   248→        pass "Sleeper PID file created: $sleeper_pid_file"
   249→
   250→        # Check if process is/was running
   251→        local pid=$(cat "$sleeper_pid_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   252→        if [ -n "$pid" ]; then
   253→            if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
   254→                pass "Sleeper process is running (PID: $pid)"
   255→            else
   256→                pass "Sleeper process completed (PID was: $pid)"
   257→            fi
   258→        else
   259→            fail "Sleeper PID file is empty"
   260→            return 1
   261→        fi
   262→    else
   263→        fail "Sleeper PID file not created (may not be implemented yet)"
   264→        return 1
   265→    fi
   266→}
   267→
   268→# Test 4: Sleeper NOT launched on subsequent calls
   269→test_sleeper_not_relaunched() {
   270→    log_test "Sleeper NOT re-launched on subsequent calls"
   271→
   272→    local session_id="test-ups-004"
   273→    create_test_session "$session_id"
   274→
   275→    # First invocation launches sleeper
   276→    invoke_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
   277→    sleep 0.2
   278→
   279→    local sleeper_pid_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/sleeper.pid"
   280→
   281→    if [ -f "$sleeper_pid_file" ]; then
   282→        local original_pid=$(cat "$sleeper_pid_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   283→
   284→        # Second invocation should NOT relaunch
   285→        invoke_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
   286→        sleep 0.2
   287→
   288→        local current_pid=$(cat "$sleeper_pid_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   289→
   290→        if [ "$original_pid" = "$current_pid" ]; then
   291→            pass "Sleeper not re-launched (PID unchanged: $original_pid)"
   292→        else
   293→            fail "Sleeper was re-launched (PID changed: $original_pid -> $current_pid)"
   294→            return 1
   295→        fi
   296→    else
   297→        fail "Sleeper not launched on first call (prerequisite failed)"
   298→        return 1
   299→    fi
   300→}
   301→
   302→# Test 5: Static reminder output at correct cadence
   303→test_static_reminder_cadence() {
   304→    log_test "Static reminder output at cadence"
   305→
   306→    local session_id="test-ups-005"
   307→    create_test_session "$session_id"
   308→
   309→    # Create a static reminder file (cadence is 4 per config)
   310→    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
   311→    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/static-reminder.txt" <<'EOF'
   312→This is a test static reminder.
   313→Remember to follow TDD principles!
   314→EOF
   315→
   316→    # Invoke 8 times, check for reminder on 4th and 8th
   317→    for i in {1..8}; do
   318→        local output=$(invoke_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" 2>&1)
   319→
   320→        if [ $i -eq 4 ] || [ $i -eq 8 ]; then
   321→            # Should output JSON with additionalContext
   322→            if echo "$output" | grep -q "additionalContext" && echo "$output" | grep -q "This is a test static reminder"; then
   323→                pass "Static reminder output on call $i (cadence matched)"
   324→            else
   325→                # Reminder feature might not be implemented yet
   326→                fail "Static reminder not output on call $i (expected, may not be implemented)"
   327→                # Don't return 1 - this is okay for TDD
   328→            fi
   329→        else
   330→            # Should not output reminder
   331→            if ! echo "$output" | grep -q "additionalContext"; then
   332→                pass "No reminder on call $i (as expected)"
   333→            else
   334→                fail "Unexpected reminder output on call $i"
   335→                return 1
   336→            fi
   337→        fi
   338→    done
   339→}
   340→
   341→# Test 6: Feature toggle - tracking disabled
   342→test_tracking_disabled() {
   343→    log_test "Feature toggle - tracking disabled"
   344→
   345→    # Disable tracking
   346→    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'
   347→FEATURE_TRACKING=false
   348→FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=false
   349→LOG_LEVEL=debug
   350→EOF
   351→
   352→    local session_id="test-ups-006"
   353→    # Don't pre-create counter since tracking is disabled
   354→    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id"
   355→
   356→    # Execute user-prompt-submit
   357→    invoke_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
   358→
   359→    # Counter should NOT be created
   360→    local counter_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/response_count"
   361→    if [ ! -f "$counter_file" ]; then
   362→        pass "Tracking disabled - counter not created"
   363→    else
   364→        fail "Tracking disabled but counter was created"
   365→        return 1
   366→    fi
   367→
   368→    # Re-enable for remaining tests
   369→    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'
   370→FEATURE_TRACKING=true
   371→FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true
   372→SLEEPER_ENABLED=true
   373→LOG_LEVEL=debug
   374→TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=5
   375→TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW=2
   376→TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE=4
   377→EOF
   378→}
   379→
   380→# Test 7: Feature toggle - topic extraction disabled
   381→test_topic_extraction_disabled() {
   382→    log_test "Feature toggle - topic extraction disabled"
   383→
   384→    # Disable topic extraction
   385→    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'
   386→FEATURE_TRACKING=true
   387→FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=false
   388→SLEEPER_ENABLED=false
   389→LOG_LEVEL=debug
   390→EOF
   391→
   392→    local session_id="test-ups-007"
   393→    create_test_session "$session_id"
   394→
   395→    # Execute user-prompt-submit
   396→    invoke_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
   397→    sleep 0.2
   398→
   399→    # Sleeper should NOT be launched
   400→    local sleeper_pid_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/sleeper.pid"
   401→    if [ ! -f "$sleeper_pid_file" ]; then
   402→        pass "Topic extraction disabled - sleeper not launched"
   403→    else
   404→        fail "Topic extraction disabled but sleeper was launched"
   405→        return 1
   406→    fi
   407→
   408→    # Re-enable for remaining tests
   409→    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'
   410→FEATURE_TRACKING=true
   411→FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true
   412→SLEEPER_ENABLED=true
   413→LOG_LEVEL=debug
   414→EOF
   415→}
   416→
   417→# Test 8: Error handling - invalid JSON
   418→test_invalid_json() {
   419→    log_test "Error handling - invalid JSON"
   420→
   421→    local invalid_json='{"session_id": "test", invalid json}'
   422→
   423→    # Execute with invalid JSON (should fail gracefully)
   424→    local output
   425→    local exit_code=0
   426→    output=$(echo "$invalid_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" user-prompt-submit 2>&1) || exit_code=$?
   427→
   428→    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
   429→        pass "user-prompt-submit failed gracefully with invalid JSON"
   430→    else
   431→        fail "user-prompt-submit should have failed with invalid JSON"
   432→        return 1
   433→    fi
   434→}
   435→
   436→# Test 9: Error handling - missing session_id
   437→test_missing_session_id() {
   438→    log_test "Error handling - missing session_id"
   439→
   440→    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
   441→{
   442→  "transcript_path": "$MOCK_TRANSCRIPT",
   443→  "workspace": {
   444→    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
   445→  }
   446→}
   447→JSON
   448→)
   449→
   450→    local output
   451→    local exit_code=0
   452→    output=$(echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" user-prompt-submit 2>&1) || exit_code=$?
   453→
   454→    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
   455→        pass "user-prompt-submit failed gracefully with missing session_id"
   456→    else
   457→        fail "user-prompt-submit should have failed with missing session_id"
   458→        return 1
   459→    fi
   460→}
   461→
   462→# Test 10: Performance - execution time under 10ms
   463→test_performance() {
   464→    log_test "Performance - execution time"
   465→
   466→    local session_id="test-ups-010"
   467→    create_test_session "$session_id"
   468→
   469→    # Measure execution time (after first call to avoid sleeper launch)
   470→    invoke_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
   471→    sleep 0.1
   472→
   473→    # Now measure second call
   474→    local start_time=$(date +%s%N)
   475→    invoke_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
   476→    local end_time=$(date +%s%N)
   477→
   478→    local duration_ms=$(( (end_time - start_time) / 1000000 ))
   479→
   480→    echo "  Execution time: ${duration_ms}ms"
   481→
   482→    # Target is <10ms for subsequent calls
   483→    if [ $duration_ms -lt 100 ]; then
   484→        pass "Execution time acceptable: ${duration_ms}ms"
   485→    else
   486→        fail "Execution time slow: ${duration_ms}ms (target <100ms)"
   487→        # Don't fail test - informational during development
   488→    fi
   489→}
   490→
   491→# Main test runner
   492→main() {
   493→    echo "=================================="
   494→    echo "Sidekick User-Prompt-Submit Integration Test"
   495→    echo "=================================="
   496→    echo ""
   497→
   498→    # Setup
   499→    setup
   500→
   501→    # Trap cleanup on exit
   502→    trap cleanup EXIT
   503→
   504→    # Run tests
   505→    echo ""
   506→    echo "Running tests..."
   507→    echo ""
   508→
   509→    test_user_prompt_submit_basic || true
   510→    test_counter_increments || true
   511→    test_sleeper_launched_on_first_call || true
   512→    test_sleeper_not_relaunched || true
   513→    test_static_reminder_cadence || true
   514→    test_tracking_disabled || true
   515→    test_topic_extraction_disabled || true
   516→    test_invalid_json || true
   517→    test_missing_session_id || true
   518→    test_performance || true
   519→
   520→    # Summary
   521→    echo ""
   522→    echo "=================================="
   523→    echo "Test Summary"
   524→    echo "=================================="
   525→    echo "Tests run:    $TESTS_RUN"
   526→    echo -e "Tests passed: ${GREEN}$TESTS_PASSED${NC}"
   527→    echo -e "Tests failed: ${RED}$TESTS_FAILED${NC}"
   528→    echo "=================================="
   529→
   530→    # Exit with failure if any tests failed
   531→    if [ $TESTS_FAILED -gt 0 ]; then
   532→        echo ""
   533→        echo -e "${RED}Some tests failed. This is expected for TDD - implement the handler to fix them.${NC}"
   534→        exit 1
   535→    else
   536→        echo ""
   537→        echo -e "${GREEN}All tests passed!${NC}"
   538→        exit 0
   539→    fi
   540→}
   541→
   542→# Run main
   543→main "$@"
   544→

#!/bin/bash
# Integration test for user-prompt-submit handler
# Tests counter increments, sleeper launch, cadence analysis, and static reminders

set -euo pipefail

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Track test results
TESTS_RUN=0
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0

# Get script directory
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../../.." && pwd)"

# Test helper functions
log_test() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}TEST:${NC} $1"
    TESTS_RUN=$((TESTS_RUN + 1))
}

pass() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}✓ PASS:${NC} $1"
    TESTS_PASSED=$((TESTS_PASSED + 1))
}

fail() {
    echo -e "${RED}✗ FAIL:${NC} $1"
    TESTS_FAILED=$((TESTS_FAILED + 1))
}

# Setup test environment
setup() {
    log_test "Setting up test environment"

    # Create temporary test directory
    TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d -t sidekick-test-XXXXXX)
    export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="$TEST_DIR"

    # Create sidekick structure in test directory
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/lib"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/handlers"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/features"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/features/prompts"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp"

    # Copy sidekick files to test directory
    cp "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/sidekick.sh" "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/"
    cp "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/lib/common.sh" "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/lib/"
    cp "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/config.defaults" "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/"

    # Copy handlers if they exist
    if [ -d "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/handlers" ]; then
        cp -r "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/handlers/"*.sh "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/handlers/" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi

    # Copy features if they exist
    if [ -d "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/features" ]; then
        cp -r "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/features/"*.sh "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/features/" 2>/dev/null || true
        cp -r "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/features/prompts/"* "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/features/prompts/" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi

    # Make sidekick.sh executable
    chmod +x "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh"

    # Create mock Claude CLI
    MOCK_CLAUDE="$TEST_DIR/mock-claude"
    cat > "$MOCK_CLAUDE" <<'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
# Mock Claude CLI for testing topic extraction
cat <<'JSON'
```json
{
  "session_id": "test-123",
  "timestamp": "2025-10-22T12:00:00Z",
  "task_ids": ["TEST-001"],
  "initial_goal": "Test goal",
  "current_objective": "Testing user prompt submit",
  "clarity_score": 7,
  "confidence": 0.85,
  "snarky_comment": "Still testing, are we?"
}
```
JSON
EOF
    chmod +x "$MOCK_CLAUDE"
    export CLAUDE_BIN="$MOCK_CLAUDE"

    # Create mock transcript file
    MOCK_TRANSCRIPT="$TEST_DIR/test-transcript.jsonl"
    cat > "$MOCK_TRANSCRIPT" <<'EOF'
{"type":"user","message":"Test user message 1"}
{"type":"assistant","message":"Test assistant response 1"}
{"type":"user","message":"Test user message 2"}
{"type":"assistant","message":"Test assistant response 2"}
EOF

    # Create test config
    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'
# Test configuration
FEATURE_TRACKING=true
FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true
FEATURE_CLEANUP=false
FEATURE_RESUME=false

# Enable sleeper for testing
SLEEPER_ENABLED=true

# Set cadence for testing
TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=5
TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW=2
TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD=7
TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE=4

# Set log level to debug
LOG_LEVEL=debug
EOF

    pass "Test environment created at $TEST_DIR"
}

# Cleanup test environment
cleanup() {
    log_test "Cleaning up test environment"

    # Kill any background processes
    if [ -n "${TEST_DIR:-}" ] && [ -d "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp" ]; then
        find "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp" -name "*.pid" -type f 2>/dev/null | while read -r pidfile; do
            if [ -f "$pidfile" ]; then
                pid=$(cat "$pidfile" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
                if [ -n "$pid" ] && kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
                    kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
                    sleep 0.1
                    kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
                fi
            fi
        done
    fi

    # Remove test directory
    if [ -n "${TEST_DIR:-}" ] && [ -d "$TEST_DIR" ]; then
        rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
        pass "Test environment cleaned up"
    fi
}

# Helper to create test session
create_test_session() {
    local session_id="$1"

    # Create session directory and counter file
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id"
    echo "0" > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/response_count"
}

# Helper to invoke user-prompt-submit hook
invoke_user_prompt_submit() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="${2:-$MOCK_TRANSCRIPT}"

    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "transcript_path": "$transcript_path",
  "workspace": {
    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
  }
}
JSON
)

    echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" user-prompt-submit 2>&1
}

# Test 1: Basic user-prompt-submit execution
test_user_prompt_submit_basic() {
    log_test "Basic user-prompt-submit execution"

    local session_id="test-ups-001"
    create_test_session "$session_id"

    # Execute user-prompt-submit
    local output
    local exit_code=0
    output=$(invoke_user_prompt_submit "$session_id") || exit_code=$?

    if [ $exit_code -eq 0 ]; then
        pass "user-prompt-submit executed without errors"
    else
        fail "user-prompt-submit failed with exit code $exit_code: $output"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test 2: Counter increments correctly
test_counter_increments() {
    log_test "Counter increments correctly"

    local session_id="test-ups-002"
    create_test_session "$session_id"

    local counter_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/response_count"

    # Invoke 5 times and check counter each time
    for i in {1..5}; do
        invoke_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

        if [ -f "$counter_file" ]; then
            local count=$(cat "$counter_file")
            if [ "$count" = "$i" ]; then
                pass "Counter correctly incremented to $i"
            else
                fail "Counter has wrong value: $count (expected $i)"
                return 1
            fi
        else
            fail "Counter file not found after invocation $i"
            return 1
        fi
    done
}

# Test 3: Sleeper launched on first call
test_sleeper_launched_on_first_call() {
    log_test "Sleeper launched on first call"

    local session_id="test-ups-003"
    create_test_session "$session_id"

    # First invocation should launch sleeper
    invoke_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    # Check if sleeper PID file was created
    local sleeper_pid_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/sleeper.pid"

    # Give it a moment to start
    sleep 0.5

    if [ -f "$sleeper_pid_file" ]; then
        pass "Sleeper PID file created: $sleeper_pid_file"

        # Check if process is/was running
        local pid=$(cat "$sleeper_pid_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        if [ -n "$pid" ]; then
            if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
                pass "Sleeper process is running (PID: $pid)"
            else
                pass "Sleeper process completed (PID was: $pid)"
            fi
        else
            fail "Sleeper PID file is empty"
            return 1
        fi
    else
        fail "Sleeper PID file not created (may not be implemented yet)"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test 4: Sleeper NOT launched on subsequent calls
test_sleeper_not_relaunched() {
    log_test "Sleeper NOT re-launched on subsequent calls"

    local session_id="test-ups-004"
    create_test_session "$session_id"

    # First invocation launches sleeper
    invoke_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    sleep 0.2

    local sleeper_pid_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/sleeper.pid"

    if [ -f "$sleeper_pid_file" ]; then
        local original_pid=$(cat "$sleeper_pid_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

        # Second invocation should NOT relaunch
        invoke_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        sleep 0.2

        local current_pid=$(cat "$sleeper_pid_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

        if [ "$original_pid" = "$current_pid" ]; then
            pass "Sleeper not re-launched (PID unchanged: $original_pid)"
        else
            fail "Sleeper was re-launched (PID changed: $original_pid -> $current_pid)"
            return 1
        fi
    else
        fail "Sleeper not launched on first call (prerequisite failed)"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test 5: Static reminder output at correct cadence
test_static_reminder_cadence() {
    log_test "Static reminder output at cadence"

    local session_id="test-ups-005"
    create_test_session "$session_id"

    # Create a static reminder file (cadence is 4 per config)
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/static-reminder.txt" <<'EOF'
This is a test static reminder.
Remember to follow TDD principles!
EOF

    # Invoke 8 times, check for reminder on 4th and 8th
    for i in {1..8}; do
        local output=$(invoke_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" 2>&1)

        if [ $i -eq 4 ] || [ $i -eq 8 ]; then
            # Should output JSON with additionalContext
            if echo "$output" | grep -q "additionalContext" && echo "$output" | grep -q "This is a test static reminder"; then
                pass "Static reminder output on call $i (cadence matched)"
            else
                # Reminder feature might not be implemented yet
                fail "Static reminder not output on call $i (expected, may not be implemented)"
                # Don't return 1 - this is okay for TDD
            fi
        else
            # Should not output reminder
            if ! echo "$output" | grep -q "additionalContext"; then
                pass "No reminder on call $i (as expected)"
            else
                fail "Unexpected reminder output on call $i"
                return 1
            fi
        fi
    done
}

# Test 6: Feature toggle - tracking disabled
test_tracking_disabled() {
    log_test "Feature toggle - tracking disabled"

    # Disable tracking
    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'
FEATURE_TRACKING=false
FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=false
LOG_LEVEL=debug
EOF

    local session_id="test-ups-006"
    # Don't pre-create counter since tracking is disabled
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id"

    # Execute user-prompt-submit
    invoke_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    # Counter should NOT be created
    local counter_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/response_count"
    if [ ! -f "$counter_file" ]; then
        pass "Tracking disabled - counter not created"
    else
        fail "Tracking disabled but counter was created"
        return 1
    fi

    # Re-enable for remaining tests
    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'
FEATURE_TRACKING=true
FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true
SLEEPER_ENABLED=true
LOG_LEVEL=debug
TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=5
TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW=2
TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE=4
EOF
}

# Test 7: Feature toggle - topic extraction disabled
test_topic_extraction_disabled() {
    log_test "Feature toggle - topic extraction disabled"

    # Disable topic extraction
    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'
FEATURE_TRACKING=true
FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=false
SLEEPER_ENABLED=false
LOG_LEVEL=debug
EOF

    local session_id="test-ups-007"
    create_test_session "$session_id"

    # Execute user-prompt-submit
    invoke_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    sleep 0.2

    # Sleeper should NOT be launched
    local sleeper_pid_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/sleeper.pid"
    if [ ! -f "$sleeper_pid_file" ]; then
        pass "Topic extraction disabled - sleeper not launched"
    else
        fail "Topic extraction disabled but sleeper was launched"
        return 1
    fi

    # Re-enable for remaining tests
    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'
FEATURE_TRACKING=true
FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true
SLEEPER_ENABLED=true
LOG_LEVEL=debug
EOF
}

# Test 8: Error handling - invalid JSON
test_invalid_json() {
    log_test "Error handling - invalid JSON"

    local invalid_json='{"session_id": "test", invalid json}'

    # Execute with invalid JSON (should fail gracefully)
    local output
    local exit_code=0
    output=$(echo "$invalid_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" user-prompt-submit 2>&1) || exit_code=$?

    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        pass "user-prompt-submit failed gracefully with invalid JSON"
    else
        fail "user-prompt-submit should have failed with invalid JSON"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test 9: Error handling - missing session_id
test_missing_session_id() {
    log_test "Error handling - missing session_id"

    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
{
  "transcript_path": "$MOCK_TRANSCRIPT",
  "workspace": {
    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
  }
}
JSON
)

    local output
    local exit_code=0
    output=$(echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" user-prompt-submit 2>&1) || exit_code=$?

    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        pass "user-prompt-submit failed gracefully with missing session_id"
    else
        fail "user-prompt-submit should have failed with missing session_id"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test 10: Performance - execution time under 10ms
test_performance() {
    log_test "Performance - execution time"

    local session_id="test-ups-010"
    create_test_session "$session_id"

    # Measure execution time (after first call to avoid sleeper launch)
    invoke_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    sleep 0.1

    # Now measure second call
    local start_time=$(date +%s%N)
    invoke_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    local end_time=$(date +%s%N)

    local duration_ms=$(( (end_time - start_time) / 1000000 ))

    echo "  Execution time: ${duration_ms}ms"

    # Target is <10ms for subsequent calls
    if [ $duration_ms -lt 100 ]; then
        pass "Execution time acceptable: ${duration_ms}ms"
    else
        fail "Execution time slow: ${duration_ms}ms (target <100ms)"
        # Don't fail test - informational during development
    fi
}

# Main test runner
main() {
    echo "=================================="
    echo "Sidekick User-Prompt-Submit Integration Test"
    echo "=================================="
    echo ""

    # Setup
    setup

    # Trap cleanup on exit
    trap cleanup EXIT

    # Run tests
    echo ""
    echo "Running tests..."
    echo ""

    test_user_prompt_submit_basic || true
    test_counter_increments || true
    test_sleeper_launched_on_first_call || true
    test_sleeper_not_relaunched || true
    test_static_reminder_cadence || true
    test_tracking_disabled || true
    test_topic_extraction_disabled || true
    test_invalid_json || true
    test_missing_session_id || true
    test_performance || true

    # Summary
    echo ""
    echo "=================================="
    echo "Test Summary"
    echo "=================================="
    echo "Tests run:    $TESTS_RUN"
    echo -e "Tests passed: ${GREEN}$TESTS_PASSED${NC}"
    echo -e "Tests failed: ${RED}$TESTS_FAILED${NC}"
    echo "=================================="

    # Exit with failure if any tests failed
    if [ $TESTS_FAILED -gt 0 ]; then
        echo ""
        echo -e "${RED}Some tests failed. This is expected for TDD - implement the handler to fix them.${NC}"
        exit 1
    else
        echo ""
        echo -e "${GREEN}All tests passed!${NC}"
        exit 0
    fi
}

# Run main
main "$@"

   296→FEATURE_TRACKING=false
   297→FEATURE_CLEANUP=false
   298→FEATURE_RESUME=false
   299→LOG_LEVEL=debug
   300→EOF
   301→

   335→}
   336→
   337→# Test 6: Resume feature (if enabled and previous session exists)
   338→test_resume_feature() {

   340→

   342→    local new_session_id="test-session-006b"

   359→{

   361→  "workspace": {

   364→  }

   366→JSON

   370→    echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

   374→    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then

   390→
   391→# Test 7: Cleanup launched in background (if enabled)
   392→test_cleanup_launched() {

   397→{

   400→    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
   401→    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
   402→  }
   403→}
   404→JSON
   405→)
   406→
   407→    # Execute session-start
   408→    echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
   409→

   411→    local cleanup_pid_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/cleanup.pid"
   412→    if [ -f "$cleanup_pid_file" ]; then

   414→
   415→        # Check if process is running
   416→        local pid=$(cat "$cleanup_pid_file")
   417→        if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
   418→            pass "Cleanup process is running (PID: $pid)"
   419→        else
   420→            # Process may have completed quickly
   421→            pass "Cleanup process completed or not running (may be normal for dry-run)"
   422→        fi
   423→    else

   425→        return 1

   428→
   429→# Test 8: Error handling - invalid JSON
   430→test_invalid_json() {

   473→}
   474→
   475→# Test 10: Performance - execution time under 100ms (target)
   476→test_performance() {

   480→    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
   481→{
   482→  "session_id": "$session_id",
   483→  "workspace": {
   484→    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
   485→    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
   486→  }
   487→}
   488→JSON
   489→)
   490→
   491→    # Measure execution time
   492→    local start_time=$(date +%s%N)
   493→    echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
   494→    local end_time=$(date +%s%N)

   496→    local duration_ms=$(( (end_time - start_time) / 1000000 ))
   497→
   498→    echo "  Execution time: ${duration_ms}ms"
   499→
   500→    # Target is <100ms, but we'll be lenient during development
   501→    # Resume feature with LLM call may take longer
   502→    if [ $duration_ms -lt 5000 ]; then
   503→        pass "Execution time acceptable: ${duration_ms}ms"
   504→    else
   505→        fail "Execution time too slow: ${duration_ms}ms (may include LLM call for resume)"
   506→        # Don't return 1 - this is informational during development
   507→    fi
   508→}
   509→
   510→# Main test runner

   519→
   520→    # Trap cleanup on exit
   521→    trap cleanup EXIT
   522→
   523→    # Run tests
   524→    echo ""
   525→    echo "Running tests..."
   526→    echo ""
   527→
   528→    test_session_start_basic || true
   529→    test_session_directory_created || true
   530→    test_tracking_initialized || true
   531→    test_log_file_created || true
   532→    test_feature_toggles || true
   533→    test_resume_feature || true
   534→    test_cleanup_launched || true
   535→    test_invalid_json || true
   536→    test_missing_session_id || true
   537→    test_performance || true

   542→    echo "Test Summary"

   546→    echo -e "Tests failed: ${RED}$TESTS_FAILED${NC}"

   548→

   551→        echo ""

   553→        exit 1
   554→    else

   556→        echo -e "${GREEN}All tests passed!${NC}"
   557→        exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# Integration test for session-start handler
# Tests the complete session-start workflow including all features

set -euo pipefail

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Track test results
TESTS_RUN=0
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0

# Get script directory
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../../.." && pwd)"

# Test helper functions
log_test() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}TEST:${NC} $1"
    TESTS_RUN=$((TESTS_RUN + 1))
}

pass() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}✓ PASS:${NC} $1"
    TESTS_PASSED=$((TESTS_PASSED + 1))
}

fail() {
    echo -e "${RED}✗ FAIL:${NC} $1"
    TESTS_FAILED=$((TESTS_FAILED + 1))
}

# Setup test environment
setup() {
    log_test "Setting up test environment"

    # Create temporary test directory
    TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d -t sidekick-test-XXXXXX)
    export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="$TEST_DIR"

    # Create sidekick structure in test directory
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/lib"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/handlers"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/features"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/features/prompts"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp"

    # Copy sidekick files to test directory
    cp "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/sidekick.sh" "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/"
    cp "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/lib/common.sh" "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/lib/"
    cp "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/config.defaults" "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/"

    # Copy handlers if they exist
    if [ -f "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/handlers/session-start.sh" ]; then
        cp "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/handlers/session-start.sh" "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/handlers/"
    fi

    # Copy features if they exist
    if [ -d "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/features" ]; then
        cp -r "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/features/"*.sh "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/features/" 2>/dev/null || true
        cp -r "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/features/prompts/"* "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/features/prompts/" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi

    # Make sidekick.sh executable
    chmod +x "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh"

    # Create mock Claude CLI
    MOCK_CLAUDE="$TEST_DIR/mock-claude"
    cat > "$MOCK_CLAUDE" <<'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
# Mock Claude CLI for testing
# Returns minimal valid JSON output

# Check if we're being called for resume snarkification
# (will have prompt containing "previous session")
if grep -q "previous session" <<< "$@" 2>/dev/null; then
    cat <<'JSON'
```json
{
  "session_id": "test-resume-123",
  "timestamp": "2025-10-22T12:00:00Z",
  "initial_goal": "Test previous session goal",
  "current_objective": "Resume testing",
  "clarity_score": 8,
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "snarky_comment": "Back for more punishment, eh?"
}
```
JSON
else
    # Default topic extraction response
    cat <<'JSON'
```json
{
  "session_id": "test-123",
  "timestamp": "2025-10-22T12:00:00Z",
  "task_ids": ["TEST-001"],
  "initial_goal": "Test goal",
  "current_objective": "Testing session start",
  "clarity_score": 7,
  "confidence": 0.85
}
```
JSON
fi
EOF
    chmod +x "$MOCK_CLAUDE"
    export CLAUDE_BIN="$MOCK_CLAUDE"

    # Create test config with all features enabled
    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'
# Test configuration with all features enabled
FEATURE_TRACKING=true
FEATURE_CLEANUP=true
FEATURE_RESUME=true
FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true
FEATURE_STATUSLINE=true

# Disable sleeper for testing (we don't want background processes during test)
SLEEPER_ENABLED=false

# Set log level to debug for testing
LOG_LEVEL=debug

# Override cleanup to dry-run for testing
CLEANUP_DRY_RUN=true
CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT=1
CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS=0
EOF

    pass "Test environment created at $TEST_DIR"
}

# Cleanup test environment
cleanup() {
    log_test "Cleaning up test environment"

    # Kill any background processes we may have started
    if [ -n "${TEST_DIR:-}" ] && [ -d "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp" ]; then
        find "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp" -name "*.pid" -type f 2>/dev/null | while read -r pidfile; do
            if [ -f "$pidfile" ]; then
                pid=$(cat "$pidfile" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
                if [ -n "$pid" ] && kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
                    kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
                fi
            fi
        done
    fi

    # Remove test directory
    if [ -n "${TEST_DIR:-}" ] && [ -d "$TEST_DIR" ]; then
        rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
        pass "Test environment cleaned up"
    fi
}

# Test 1: Basic session-start execution
test_session_start_basic() {
    log_test "Basic session-start execution"

    local session_id="test-session-001"
    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "workspace": {
    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
  }
}
JSON
)

    # Execute session-start
    local output
    if output=$(echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start 2>&1); then
        pass "session-start executed without errors"
    else
        fail "session-start failed with exit code $?: $output"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test 2: Session directory creation
test_session_directory_created() {
    log_test "Session directory creation"

    local session_id="test-session-002"
    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "workspace": {
    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
  }
}
JSON
)

    # Execute session-start
    echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    # Check if session directory was created
    local session_dir="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id"
    if [ -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        pass "Session directory created: $session_dir"
    else
        fail "Session directory not created: $session_dir"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test 3: Tracking counter initialization (if enabled)
test_tracking_initialized() {
    log_test "Tracking counter initialization"

    local session_id="test-session-003"
    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "workspace": {
    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
  }
}
JSON
)

    # Execute session-start
    echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    # Check if counter file was created
    local counter_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/response_count"
    if [ -f "$counter_file" ]; then
        local count=$(cat "$counter_file")
        if [ "$count" = "0" ]; then
            pass "Tracking counter initialized to 0"
        else
            fail "Tracking counter has wrong value: $count (expected 0)"
            return 1
        fi
    else
        fail "Tracking counter file not created: $counter_file"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test 4: Log file creation
test_log_file_created() {
    log_test "Log file creation"

    local session_id="test-session-004"
    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "workspace": {
    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
  }
}
JSON
)

    # Execute session-start
    echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    # Check if log file was created
    local log_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/sidekick.log"
    if [ -f "$log_file" ]; then
        pass "Log file created: $log_file"

        # Check log file has content
        if [ -s "$log_file" ]; then
            pass "Log file has content"
        else
            fail "Log file is empty"
            return 1
        fi
    else
        fail "Log file not created: $log_file"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test 5: Feature toggles respected
test_feature_toggles() {
    log_test "Feature toggles respected"

    # Create config with tracking disabled
    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'
FEATURE_TRACKING=false
FEATURE_CLEANUP=false
FEATURE_RESUME=false
LOG_LEVEL=debug
EOF

    local session_id="test-session-005"
    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "workspace": {
    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
  }
}
JSON
)

    # Execute session-start
    echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    # Check that counter file was NOT created (tracking disabled)
    local counter_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/response_count"
    if [ ! -f "$counter_file" ]; then
        pass "Tracking disabled - counter file not created"
    else
        fail "Tracking disabled but counter file was created"
        return 1
    fi

    # Re-enable features for remaining tests
    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'
FEATURE_TRACKING=true
FEATURE_CLEANUP=true
FEATURE_RESUME=true
LOG_LEVEL=debug
SLEEPER_ENABLED=false
CLEANUP_DRY_RUN=true
EOF
}

# Test 6: Resume feature (if enabled and previous session exists)
test_resume_feature() {
    log_test "Resume feature"

    local prev_session_id="test-session-006a"
    local new_session_id="test-session-006b"

    # Create a previous session with a topic file
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$prev_session_id"
    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$prev_session_id/topic.json" <<'EOF'
{
  "session_id": "test-session-006a",
  "timestamp": "2025-10-22T10:00:00Z",
  "initial_goal": "Previous session goal",
  "current_objective": "Testing resume",
  "clarity_score": 8,
  "confidence": 0.9
}
EOF

    # Start new session
    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
{
  "session_id": "$new_session_id",
  "workspace": {
    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
  }
}
JSON
)

    # Execute session-start
    echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    # Check if new session has topic file (created by resume feature)
    local topic_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$new_session_id/topic.json"
    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        pass "Resume created topic file for new session"

        # Verify it contains resume information
        if grep -q "snarky_comment" "$topic_file"; then
            pass "Topic file contains snarky resume comment"
        else
            # This might be okay - snarky_comment is optional
            pass "Topic file created (snarky comment optional)"
        fi
    else
        # This might fail if resume feature is not implemented yet
        fail "Resume topic file not created: $topic_file (may not be implemented yet)"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test 7: Cleanup launched in background (if enabled)
test_cleanup_launched() {
    log_test "Cleanup launched in background"

    local session_id="test-session-007"
    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "workspace": {
    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
  }
}
JSON
)

    # Execute session-start
    echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    # Check if cleanup PID file was created
    local cleanup_pid_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/cleanup.pid"
    if [ -f "$cleanup_pid_file" ]; then
        pass "Cleanup PID file created: $cleanup_pid_file"

        # Check if process is running
        local pid=$(cat "$cleanup_pid_file")
        if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
            pass "Cleanup process is running (PID: $pid)"
        else
            # Process may have completed quickly
            pass "Cleanup process completed or not running (may be normal for dry-run)"
        fi
    else
        fail "Cleanup PID file not created (may not be implemented yet)"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test 8: Error handling - invalid JSON
test_invalid_json() {
    log_test "Error handling - invalid JSON"

    local invalid_json='{"session_id": "test", invalid json}'

    # Execute session-start with invalid JSON (should fail gracefully)
    local output
    local exit_code=0
    output=$(echo "$invalid_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start 2>&1) || exit_code=$?

    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        pass "session-start failed gracefully with invalid JSON (exit code: $exit_code)"
    else
        fail "session-start should have failed with invalid JSON but succeeded"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test 9: Error handling - missing session_id
test_missing_session_id() {
    log_test "Error handling - missing session_id"

    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
{
  "workspace": {
    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
  }
}
JSON
)

    # Execute session-start without session_id (should fail gracefully)
    local output
    local exit_code=0
    output=$(echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start 2>&1) || exit_code=$?

    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        pass "session-start failed gracefully with missing session_id (exit code: $exit_code)"
    else
        fail "session-start should have failed with missing session_id but succeeded"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test 10: Performance - execution time under 100ms (target)
test_performance() {
    log_test "Performance - execution time"

    local session_id="test-session-010"
    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "workspace": {
    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
  }
}
JSON
)

    # Measure execution time
    local start_time=$(date +%s%N)
    echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    local end_time=$(date +%s%N)

    local duration_ms=$(( (end_time - start_time) / 1000000 ))

    echo "  Execution time: ${duration_ms}ms"

    # Target is <100ms, but we'll be lenient during development
    # Resume feature with LLM call may take longer
    if [ $duration_ms -lt 5000 ]; then
        pass "Execution time acceptable: ${duration_ms}ms"
    else
        fail "Execution time too slow: ${duration_ms}ms (may include LLM call for resume)"
        # Don't return 1 - this is informational during development
    fi
}

# Main test runner
main() {
    echo "=================================="
    echo "Sidekick Session-Start Integration Test"
    echo "=================================="
    echo ""

    # Setup
    setup

    # Trap cleanup on exit
    trap cleanup EXIT

    # Run tests
    echo ""
    echo "Running tests..."
    echo ""

    test_session_start_basic || true
    test_session_directory_created || true
    test_tracking_initialized || true
    test_log_file_created || true
    test_feature_toggles || true
    test_resume_feature || true
    test_cleanup_launched || true
    test_invalid_json || true
    test_missing_session_id || true
    test_performance || true

    # Summary
    echo ""
    echo "=================================="
    echo "Test Summary"
    echo "=================================="
    echo "Tests run:    $TESTS_RUN"
    echo -e "Tests passed: ${GREEN}$TESTS_PASSED${NC}"
    echo -e "Tests failed: ${RED}$TESTS_FAILED${NC}"
    echo "=================================="

    # Exit with failure if any tests failed
    if [ $TESTS_FAILED -gt 0 ]; then
        echo ""
        echo -e "${RED}Some tests failed. This is expected for TDD - implement the handler to fix them.${NC}"
        exit 1
    else
        echo ""
        echo -e "${GREEN}All tests passed!${NC}"
        exit 0
    fi
}

# Run main
main "$@"

#!/bin/bash
# test-workspace.sh - Unit tests for workspace management functions
#
# Tests the WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT namespace from lib/common.sh

set -euo pipefail

# Colors for test output
readonly GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly RESET='\033[0m'

# Test counters
TESTS_RUN=0
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0

# Setup test environment
setup() {
    TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d)

    # Mock LOG_LEVEL to suppress debug output
    export LOG_LEVEL=error

    # Source common.sh
    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    source "$(dirname "$0")/../../../src/sidekick/lib/common.sh" 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Teardown test environment
teardown() {
    # Clean up any workspaces created during tests
    rm -rf /tmp/sidekick-* 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
}

# Test helper
run_test() {
    local test_name="$1"
    TESTS_RUN=$((TESTS_RUN + 1))

    if "$test_name"; then
        echo -e "${GREEN}✓${RESET} ${test_name}"
        TESTS_PASSED=$((TESTS_PASSED + 1))
        return 0
    else
        echo -e "${RED}✗${RESET} ${test_name}"
        TESTS_FAILED=$((TESTS_FAILED + 1))
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test: workspace_create creates directory
test_workspace_create_directory() {
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "test-$$")

    # Directory should exist
    [ -d "$workspace" ]

    # Cleanup
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
}

# Test: workspace_create has correct path pattern
test_workspace_create_path_pattern() {
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "test-123")

    # Should be in /tmp and contain identifier
    [[ "$workspace" == /tmp/sidekick-test-123 ]]

    # Cleanup
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
}

# Test: workspace_create creates settings file
test_workspace_create_settings_file() {
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "test-$$")

    local settings="${workspace}/.claude.settings.json"
    [ -f "$settings" ]

    # Cleanup
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
}

# Test: workspace_create settings disable hooks
test_workspace_create_disables_hooks() {
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "test-$$")

    local settings="${workspace}/.claude.settings.json"

    # Verify hooks are disabled
    local hooks
    hooks=$(jq -r '.hooks' "$settings")
    [ "$hooks" = "{}" ]

    # Verify statusLine is disabled
    local statusline
    statusline=$(jq -r '.statusLine.enabled' "$settings")
    [ "$statusline" = "false" ]

    # Cleanup
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
}

# Test: workspace_cleanup removes directory
test_workspace_cleanup_removes_directory() {
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "test-cleanup-$$")

    # Verify it exists
    [ -d "$workspace" ]

    # Cleanup
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    # Should be removed
    [ ! -d "$workspace" ]
}

# Test: workspace_cleanup handles non-existent directory
test_workspace_cleanup_nonexistent() {
    local workspace="/tmp/nonexistent-workspace-$$"

    # Should not error
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
}

# Test: workspace_create creates isolated environment
test_workspace_create_isolation() {
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "test-isolation-$$")

    # Create a test file in workspace
    touch "${workspace}/test-file.txt"
    [ -f "${workspace}/test-file.txt" ]

    # Cleanup should remove everything
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
    [ ! -f "${workspace}/test-file.txt" ]
}

# Test: multiple workspaces can coexist
test_multiple_workspaces() {
    local workspace1
    local workspace2

    workspace1=$(workspace_create "test-multi-1-$$")
    workspace2=$(workspace_create "test-multi-2-$$")

    # Both should exist
    [ -d "$workspace1" ]
    [ -d "$workspace2" ]

    # Should have different paths
    [ "$workspace1" != "$workspace2" ]

    # Cleanup
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace1"
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace2"

    # Both should be removed
    [ ! -d "$workspace1" ]
    [ ! -d "$workspace2" ]
}

# Main test execution
main() {
    echo "Running workspace management namespace tests..."
    echo

    setup

    # Run all tests
    run_test test_workspace_create_directory
    run_test test_workspace_create_path_pattern
    run_test test_workspace_create_settings_file
    run_test test_workspace_create_disables_hooks
    run_test test_workspace_cleanup_removes_directory
    run_test test_workspace_cleanup_nonexistent
    run_test test_workspace_create_isolation
    run_test test_multiple_workspaces

    teardown

    # Print summary
    echo
    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
    echo "Tests run:    ${TESTS_RUN}"
    echo -e "Tests passed: ${GREEN}${TESTS_PASSED}${RESET}"
    if [ $TESTS_FAILED -gt 0 ]; then
        echo -e "Tests failed: ${RED}${TESTS_FAILED}${RESET}"
        exit 1
    else
        echo -e "${GREEN}All tests passed!${RESET}"
        exit 0
    fi
}

main "$@"

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# test-process.sh - Unit tests for process management functions
     3→#
     4→# Tests the PROCESS MANAGEMENT namespace from lib/common.sh
     5→
     6→set -euo pipefail
     7→
     8→# Colors for test output
     9→readonly GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    10→readonly RED='\033[0;31m'
    11→readonly RESET='\033[0m'
    12→
    13→# Test counters
    14→TESTS_RUN=0
    15→TESTS_PASSED=0
    16→TESTS_FAILED=0
    17→
    18→# Setup test environment
    19→setup() {
    20→    TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
    21→
    22→    # Mock path functions for testing
    23→    path_get_sidekick_root() {
    24→        echo "${TEST_DIR}"
    25→    }
    26→    export -f path_get_sidekick_root
    27→
    28→    path_get_session_dir() {
    29→        local session_id="$1"
    30→        local session_dir="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}"
    31→        mkdir -p "$session_dir"
    32→        echo "$session_dir"
    33→    }
    34→    export -f path_get_session_dir
    35→
    36→    # Source common.sh
    37→    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    38→    source "$(dirname "$0")/../../../src/sidekick/lib/common.sh" 2>/dev/null || true
    39→}
    40→
    41→# Teardown test environment
    42→teardown() {
    43→    # Kill any test processes
    44→    find "$TEST_DIR" -name "*.pid" -type f 2>/dev/null | while read -r pid_file; do
    45→        if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
    46→            local pid
    47→            pid=$(cat "$pid_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    48→            if [ -n "$pid" ]; then
    49→                kill -KILL "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
    50→            fi
    51→        fi
    52→    done
    53→
    54→    rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
    55→}
    56→
    57→# Test helper
    58→run_test() {
    59→    local test_name="$1"
    60→    TESTS_RUN=$((TESTS_RUN + 1))
    61→
    62→    if "$test_name"; then
    63→        echo -e "${GREEN}✓${RESET} ${test_name}"
    64→        TESTS_PASSED=$((TESTS_PASSED + 1))
    65→        return 0
    66→    else
    67→        echo -e "${RED}✗${RESET} ${test_name}"
    68→        TESTS_FAILED=$((TESTS_FAILED + 1))
    69→        return 1
    70→    fi
    71→}
    72→
    73→# Dummy function for background process testing
    74→test_dummy_function() {
    75→    local sleep_time="${1:-1}"
    76→    echo "Background process running"
    77→    sleep "$sleep_time"
    78→    echo "Background process complete"
    79→}
    80→export -f test_dummy_function
    81→
    82→# Test: process_launch_background creates PID file
    83→test_process_launch_background_creates_pid() {
    84→    local session_id="test-pid-$$"
    85→
    86→    process_launch_background "$session_id" "testproc" test_dummy_function 1
    87→
    88→    local pid_file="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}/testproc.pid"
    89→    [ -f "$pid_file" ]
    90→
    91→    # Cleanup
    92→    process_kill "$pid_file"
    93→}
    94→
    95→# Test: process_launch_background creates log file
    96→test_process_launch_background_creates_log() {
    97→    local session_id="test-log-$$"
    98→
    99→    process_launch_background "$session_id" "testproc" test_dummy_function 1
   100→
   101→    local log_file="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}/testproc.log"
   102→    sleep 0.5  # Give it a moment to start
   103→
   104→    [ -f "$log_file" ]
   105→
   106→    # Cleanup
   107→    local pid_file="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}/testproc.pid"
   108→    process_kill "$pid_file"
   109→}
   110→
   111→# Test: process_launch_background doesn't launch if already running
   112→test_process_launch_background_prevents_duplicate() {
   113→    local session_id="test-dup-$$"
   114→
   115→    # Launch first process
   116→    process_launch_background "$session_id" "testproc" test_dummy_function 5
   117→
   118→    local pid_file="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}/testproc.pid"
   119→    local pid1
   120→    pid1=$(cat "$pid_file")
   121→
   122→    # Try to launch again
   123→    process_launch_background "$session_id" "testproc" test_dummy_function 5
   124→
   125→    local pid2
   126→    pid2=$(cat "$pid_file")
   127→
   128→    # PID should be the same (didn't launch new process)
   129→    [ "$pid1" = "$pid2" ]
   130→
   131→    # Cleanup
   132→    process_kill "$pid_file"
   133→}
   134→
   135→# Test: process_is_running returns 0 for running process
   136→test_process_is_running_true() {
   137→    local session_id="test-running-$$"
   138→
   139→    process_launch_background "$session_id" "testproc" test_dummy_function 5
   140→
   141→    local pid_file="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}/testproc.pid"
   142→    sleep 0.5  # Give it a moment to start
   143→
   144→    process_is_running "$pid_file"
   145→
   146→    # Cleanup
   147→    process_kill "$pid_file"
   148→}
   149→
   150→# Test: process_is_running returns 1 for non-existent file
   151→test_process_is_running_no_file() {
   152→    local pid_file="${TEST_DIR}/nonexistent.pid"
   153→
   154→    ! process_is_running "$pid_file"
   155→}
   156→
   157→# Test: process_is_running returns 1 for dead process
   158→test_process_is_running_dead_process() {
   159→    local session_id="test-dead-$$"
   160→    local pid_file="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}/testproc.pid"
   161→
   162→    mkdir -p "${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}"
   163→
   164→    # Create PID file with non-existent PID
   165→    echo "999999" > "$pid_file"
   166→
   167→    ! process_is_running "$pid_file"
   168→}
   169→
   170→# Test: process_kill stops running process
   171→test_process_kill_stops_process() {
   172→    local session_id="test-kill-$$"
   173→
   174→    process_launch_background "$session_id" "testproc" test_dummy_function 60
   175→
   176→    local pid_file="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}/testproc.pid"
   177→    sleep 0.5  # Let it start
   178→
   179→    # Verify it's running
   180→    process_is_running "$pid_file"
   181→
   182→    # Kill it
   183→    process_kill "$pid_file"
   184→
   185→    # Should no longer be running
   186→    ! process_is_running "$pid_file"
   187→
   188→    # PID file should be removed
   189→    [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]
   190→}
   191→
   192→# Test: process_kill removes PID file
   193→test_process_kill_removes_pid_file() {
   194→    local session_id="test-kill-pid-$$"
   195→
   196→    process_launch_background "$session_id" "testproc" test_dummy_function 5
   197→
   198→    local pid_file="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}/testproc.pid"
   199→    sleep 0.5
   200→
   201→    process_kill "$pid_file"
   202→
   203→    [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]
   204→}
   205→
   206→# Test: process_cleanup_stale_pids removes stale PIDs
   207→test_process_cleanup_stale_pids() {

#!/bin/bash
# test-process.sh - Unit tests for process management functions
#
# Tests the PROCESS MANAGEMENT namespace from lib/common.sh

set -euo pipefail

# Colors for test output
readonly GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly RESET='\033[0m'

# Test counters
TESTS_RUN=0
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0

# Setup test environment
setup() {
    TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d)

    # Mock path functions for testing
    path_get_sidekick_root() {
        echo "${TEST_DIR}"
    }
    export -f path_get_sidekick_root

    path_get_session_dir() {
        local session_id="$1"
        local session_dir="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}"
        mkdir -p "$session_dir"
        echo "$session_dir"
    }
    export -f path_get_session_dir

    # Source common.sh
    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    source "$(dirname "$0")/../../../src/sidekick/lib/common.sh" 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Teardown test environment
teardown() {
    # Kill any test processes
    find "$TEST_DIR" -name "*.pid" -type f 2>/dev/null | while read -r pid_file; do
        if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
            local pid
            pid=$(cat "$pid_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
            if [ -n "$pid" ]; then
                kill -KILL "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
            fi
        fi
    done

    rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
}

# Test helper
run_test() {
    local test_name="$1"
    TESTS_RUN=$((TESTS_RUN + 1))

    if "$test_name"; then
        echo -e "${GREEN}✓${RESET} ${test_name}"
        TESTS_PASSED=$((TESTS_PASSED + 1))
        return 0
    else
        echo -e "${RED}✗${RESET} ${test_name}"
        TESTS_FAILED=$((TESTS_FAILED + 1))
        return 1
    fi
}

# Dummy function for background process testing
test_dummy_function() {
    local sleep_time="${1:-1}"
    echo "Background process running"
    sleep "$sleep_time"
    echo "Background process complete"
}
export -f test_dummy_function

# Test: process_launch_background creates PID file
test_process_launch_background_creates_pid() {
    local session_id="test-pid-$$"

    process_launch_background "$session_id" "testproc" test_dummy_function 1

    local pid_file="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}/testproc.pid"
    [ -f "$pid_file" ]

    # Cleanup
    process_kill "$pid_file"
}

# Test: process_launch_background creates log file
test_process_launch_background_creates_log() {
    local session_id="test-log-$$"

    process_launch_background "$session_id" "testproc" test_dummy_function 1

    local log_file="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}/testproc.log"
    sleep 0.5  # Give it a moment to start

    [ -f "$log_file" ]

    # Cleanup
    local pid_file="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}/testproc.pid"
    process_kill "$pid_file"
}

# Test: process_launch_background doesn't launch if already running
test_process_launch_background_prevents_duplicate() {
    local session_id="test-dup-$$"

    # Launch first process
    process_launch_background "$session_id" "testproc" test_dummy_function 5

    local pid_file="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}/testproc.pid"
    local pid1
    pid1=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Try to launch again
    process_launch_background "$session_id" "testproc" test_dummy_function 5

    local pid2
    pid2=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # PID should be the same (didn't launch new process)
    [ "$pid1" = "$pid2" ]

    # Cleanup
    process_kill "$pid_file"
}

# Test: process_is_running returns 0 for running process
test_process_is_running_true() {
    local session_id="test-running-$$"

    process_launch_background "$session_id" "testproc" test_dummy_function 5

    local pid_file="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}/testproc.pid"
    sleep 0.5  # Give it a moment to start

    process_is_running "$pid_file"

    # Cleanup
    process_kill "$pid_file"
}

# Test: process_is_running returns 1 for non-existent file
test_process_is_running_no_file() {
    local pid_file="${TEST_DIR}/nonexistent.pid"

    ! process_is_running "$pid_file"
}

# Test: process_is_running returns 1 for dead process
test_process_is_running_dead_process() {
    local session_id="test-dead-$$"
    local pid_file="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}/testproc.pid"

    mkdir -p "${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}"

    # Create PID file with non-existent PID
    echo "999999" > "$pid_file"

    ! process_is_running "$pid_file"
}

# Test: process_kill stops running process
test_process_kill_stops_process() {
    local session_id="test-kill-$$"

    process_launch_background "$session_id" "testproc" test_dummy_function 60

    local pid_file="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}/testproc.pid"
    sleep 0.5  # Let it start

    # Verify it's running
    process_is_running "$pid_file"

    # Kill it
    process_kill "$pid_file"

    # Should no longer be running
    ! process_is_running "$pid_file"

    # PID file should be removed
    [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]
}

# Test: process_kill removes PID file
test_process_kill_removes_pid_file() {
    local session_id="test-kill-pid-$$"

    process_launch_background "$session_id" "testproc" test_dummy_function 5

    local pid_file="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}/testproc.pid"
    sleep 0.5

    process_kill "$pid_file"

    [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]
}

# Test: process_cleanup_stale_pids removes stale PIDs
test_process_cleanup_stale_pids() {
    local session_id="test-cleanup-$$"
    local session_dir="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}"
    mkdir -p "$session_dir"

    # Create stale PID file
    echo "999999" > "${session_dir}/stale.pid"

    # Create active process
    process_launch_background "$session_id" "active" test_dummy_function 5
    sleep 0.5

    # Cleanup stale PIDs
    process_cleanup_stale_pids "$session_dir"

    # Stale PID should be removed
    [ ! -f "${session_dir}/stale.pid" ]

    # Active PID should remain
    [ -f "${session_dir}/active.pid" ]

    # Cleanup
    process_kill "${session_dir}/active.pid"
}

# Test: process_cleanup_stale_pids handles empty directory
test_process_cleanup_stale_pids_empty_dir() {
    local session_dir="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/empty-$$"
    mkdir -p "$session_dir"

    # Should not error on empty directory
    process_cleanup_stale_pids "$session_dir"
}

# Test: process_cleanup_stale_pids handles non-existent directory
test_process_cleanup_stale_pids_nonexistent() {
    local session_dir="${TEST_DIR}/nonexistent-$$"

    # Should not error on non-existent directory
    process_cleanup_stale_pids "$session_dir"
}

# Main test execution
main() {
    echo "Running process management namespace tests..."
    echo

    setup

    # Run all tests
    run_test test_process_launch_background_creates_pid
    run_test test_process_launch_background_creates_log
    run_test test_process_launch_background_prevents_duplicate
    run_test test_process_is_running_true
    run_test test_process_is_running_no_file
    run_test test_process_is_running_dead_process
    run_test test_process_kill_stops_process
    run_test test_process_kill_removes_pid_file
    run_test test_process_cleanup_stale_pids
    run_test test_process_cleanup_stale_pids_empty_dir
    run_test test_process_cleanup_stale_pids_nonexistent

    teardown

    # Print summary
    echo
    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
    echo "Tests run:    ${TESTS_RUN}"
    echo -e "Tests passed: ${GREEN}${TESTS_PASSED}${RESET}"
    if [ $TESTS_FAILED -gt 0 ]; then
        echo -e "Tests failed: ${RED}${TESTS_FAILED}${RESET}"
        exit 1
    else
        echo -e "${GREEN}All tests passed!${RESET}"
        exit 0
    fi
}

main "$@"

# ============================================================================
# PATH RESOLUTION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Detect execution scope (user or project)
# Determines if running from ~/.claude or project .claude
# Returns:
#   "user" or "project"
path_detect_scope() {
    local script_path
    script_path=$(readlink -f "${BASH_SOURCE[1]}")

    if [[ "$script_path" == "${HOME}/.claude"* ]]; then
        echo "user"
    else
        echo "project"
    fi
}

# Get sidekick installation directory
# Returns absolute path to sidekick root (contains sidekick.sh)
# Globals:
#   _SIDEKICK_ROOT - cached value
path_get_sidekick_root() {
    # Return cached value if available
    if [ -n "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT" ]; then
        echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
        return 0
    fi

    # Determine root from this script's location
    # This file is at: {root}/lib/common.sh
    local script_dir
    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
    _SIDEKICK_ROOT=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)

    echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
}

# Get session-specific directory (creates if missing)
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to session directory: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/{session-id}/
# Notes:
#   - CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR must be set (Sidekick requires project context)
#   - Session state is always stored at project root in .sidekick/sessions/
#   - This directory is gitignored and contains ephemeral session state
path_get_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"

    # Require CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR - Sidekick only works in project contexts
    if [ -z "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        log_error "CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR is not set. Sidekick requires a project context."
        return 1
    fi

    # Use project-local session directory
    local session_dir="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}"

    # Create if doesn't exist
    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        mkdir -p "$session_dir"
    fi

    echo "$session_dir"
}

# Args:
#   $1 - JSON input (optional, reads from stdin if empty)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to project directory
path_get_project_dir() {
    local json="${1:-}"

    # If JSON provided, try to extract from it
    if [ -n "$json" ]; then
        local from_json

        if [ -n "$from_json" ]; then
